fileSummaries.lib/portal-config.ts: This file manages portal configuration settings for AI agents, retrieving per-firm settings from Redis and compiling them into structured system prompt instructions. It sits between the data layer and AI prompt construction, translating stored configuration (personality, goals, skill packs, writing style) into directive text blocks that shape how the agent communicates with clients.
fileSummaries.CLAUDE.md: This is a developer guidance file for Claude Code AI assistant, documenting the architecture, tech stack, key files, and mandatory conventions for a Next.js 15 project that hosts "Rex" (an embeddable AI chat widget for client websites) and "Mia" (Saabai's own AI widget), with the critical rule that every Rex change must be logged in the changelog file before committing.
fileSummaries.lib/lex-knowledge.ts: This file defines the full static knowledge base injected into Lex, Tributum Law's AI assistant, covering Australian and international tax law, residency rules, estate planning, asset protection, and firm-specific context including contact details, lead qualification signals, and disclaimer frameworks. It serves as the single source of truth for Lex's legal domain expertise, designed for full-context injection with Anthropic prompt caching to minimize latency compared to tool-based retrieval.
fileSummaries.lib/rex-knowledge.ts: This file defines Rex's complete knowledge base for PlasticOnline, a plastics supplier, containing structured information about the company, products, materials, and FAQs across seven topic modules. It serves as the full-context system prompt injection for the Rex AI sales assistant, architected to support future migration to retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) when content exceeds the current prompt-caching threshold.
fileSummaries.lib/rex-pricing-engine.ts: This file is the deterministic pricing engine for PlasticOnline's AI assistant (Rex), providing a single `getPricing()` function that calculates exact prices for sheets, rods, and tubes across all stocked plastic materials using hardcoded data tables rather than LLM arithmetic. It handles business rules including cut-to-size rates, full sheet pricing, bulk discounts, and minimum order fees, and is invoked as a tool call by Rex to return structured price results with product URLs.
fileSummaries.README.md: This is the standard boilerplate README for a Next.js project bootstrapped with create-next-app, providing developer onboarding instructions for running the dev server, editing pages, and deploying to Vercel. It serves as the project's entry-point documentation with no custom business logic, indicating this is likely an early-stage or template-based Next.js frontend application.
fileSummaries.lib/analytics.ts: This file is a thin client-side analytics utility for the chat widget that tracks key funnel events (bubble shown, widget opened, lead qualified, CTA clicked, etc.) by POSTing to an internal /api/analytics endpoint in a fire-and-forget manner. It acts as an abstraction layer between the widget components and any third-party analytics provider (PostHog, GA4, Segment), centralizing all event tracking logic in one place for easy future wiring.
fileSummaries.lib/chat-config.ts: This file centralizes AI language model configuration for the application, resolving provider-specific model instances (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) from environment variables using a "provider:model-id" format to support multi-tier and multi-tenant chat use cases. It exports model selectors for two distinct chat contexts (Rex sales assistant and Saabai's Mia/Atlas) with default/premium tiers, plus a heuristic function that analyzes conversation content and length to dynamically escalate from a cost-efficient model to a high-capability one mid-conversation.
fileSummaries.lib/lex-config.ts: This file is the central configuration registry for Lex, Saabai's AI legal assistant, defining two operational modes per law firm client: an internal lawyer-facing research and drafting assistant with full legal tool access, and an external client-facing intake assistant restricted to lead capture. It exports TypeScript types, system prompts, tool sets, and a getLexConfig() lookup function that routes incoming clientId parameters from the chat UI or widget to the correct firm-specific configuration at runtime.
fileSummaries.lib/rex-config.ts: This file is the multi-client configuration registry for the Rex AI assistant, defining per-client system prompts, enabled tools, email settings, quick replies, and branding for each business (e.g. PlasticOnline). It serves as the single source of truth that the rest of the Rex system reads via getClientConfig() to customize agent behavior based on the clientId passed through the widget and API request chain.
fileSummaries.app/api/analytics/route.ts: Edge API route that acts as an analytics event sink, receiving tracking events from lib/analytics.ts via HTTP POST and logging them to console with placeholder wiring for third-party providers like PostHog, GA4, or Segment. It sits at the boundary between the client-side analytics abstraction layer and external analytics infrastructure, currently serving as a passthrough stub ready for provider integration.
fileSummaries.app/api/rex-analytics/realtime/route.ts: This is an Edge API route that serves real-time performance metrics for the Rex lead monitoring dashboard, pulling counters and response time lists from Upstash Redis to compute conversion rates, P95/P99 latency percentiles, and a GREEN/YELLOW/RED health status. It also exports a trackResponseTime utility used by other parts of the system to record per-request durations into Redis for rolling percentile calculations.
fileSummaries.lib/portal-session.ts: This file implements stateless, cookie-based session management for the Saabai Client Portal using HMAC-SHA256 signed tokens that encode the user's email and expiry without any server-side session store. It provides two functions — signSession to create a 7-day token and verifySession to validate and decode it — sitting at the authentication layer between portal login and protected route middleware.
fileSummaries.app/api/cron/linkedin-scheduler/route.ts: This is a cron job API route that runs daily at 11pm UTC to automatically publish scheduled LinkedIn posts by fetching pending posts from Redis, filtering for those due on or before the current Brisbane AEST date, and forwarding them to a Make.com webhook that handles the actual LinkedIn posting. It sits at the intersection of the scheduling infrastructure and the social media publishing pipeline, acting as the timed trigger that moves posts from the Redis queue to distribution.
fileSummaries.app/api/instagram/cron/route.ts: This is a Vercel Cron job endpoint that runs on a schedule to publish pending Instagram posts by retrieving due posts from Redis and pushing them to the Instagram Graph API using a two-step container-then-publish flow. It sits in the scheduled automation layer of the system, bridging the internal Redis post queue with the external Instagram Business API, and marks each post as sent upon successful publication.
fileSummaries.app/api/instagram/generate/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route that accepts a topic, format, and notes, then calls the Anthropic Claude API to generate branded Instagram captions for Saabai.ai using a strict prompt defining Shane Goldberg's voice, formatting rules, and content style. It sits in the content generation layer of the system, serving as the backend endpoint for an Instagram caption creation tool targeted at Australian professional services firms.
fileSummaries.app/api/instagram/post/route.ts: This Next.js API route handles publishing image posts to Instagram by executing the two-step Instagram Graph API flow: first creating a media container with the provided image URL and caption, then publishing it to the configured business account. It sits in the backend API layer as the system's Instagram publishing endpoint, consuming credentials from environment variables and returning the resulting post ID on success.
fileSummaries.app/api/instagram/queue/route.ts: This Next.js API route manages the Instagram post scheduling queue, providing CRUD operations (GET pending/sent posts, POST to queue new posts, PATCH to edit queued posts, DELETE/cancel posts) backed by Redis storage. It serves as the central REST endpoint for the Instagram scheduling system, sitting between the frontend scheduler UI and the Redis-backed queue that a cron job consumes to publish posts.
fileSummaries.app/api/linkedin/generate/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route that generates LinkedIn posts for a specific person (Shane Goldberg) by sending structured prompts to Anthropic's Claude API, enforcing a tightly defined personal voice, formatting rules, and CTA guidelines. It sits in the content generation layer of what appears to be a LinkedIn ghostwriting tool, accepting a post format, topic, and optional notes, then returning ready-to-publish post text.
fileSummaries.app/api/linkedin/post/route.ts: This is a Next.js Edge API route that accepts authenticated POST requests to publish or schedule LinkedIn posts by forwarding the content, optional OG image URL, and scheduling metadata to a Make.com webhook. It sits in the admin/content publishing layer of the system, bridging the saabai.ai backend with LinkedIn automation via Make.com.
fileSummaries.app/api/linkedin/queue/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route that manages a LinkedIn post scheduling queue, providing CRUD operations to create, retrieve, edit, and cancel scheduled LinkedIn posts stored in Redis. It sits between the frontend scheduling UI and the Redis data layer, serving as the REST interface consumed by the cron job and admin tools to manage post queues and history.
fileSummaries.app/api/og/linkedin-card/route.tsx: This is an Edge API route that dynamically generates branded Open Graph images sized for LinkedIn (1200x627px) by rendering JSX to PNG via Next.js ImageResponse. It supports four card layouts (stat, insight, quote, beforeafter) driven by URL query parameters, serving as the social media image generation layer for Saabai.ai's marketing content.
fileSummaries.app/api/portal/auth/route.ts: This is the magic link authentication callback endpoint for the client portal, which validates a one-time-use token from Redis, exchanges it for a 7-day HMAC-signed session cookie, and redirects the user to the portal via a 200 HTML response to ensure reliable cookie setting across browsers and CDNs.
fileSummaries.app/api/portal/login/route.ts: This Next.js API route handles passwordless authentication for the Saabai client portal by generating a 32-character token, storing it in Redis with a 15-minute TTL, and emailing a magic sign-in link to the requesting firm via Resend. It serves as the entry point of the portal's auth flow, sitting between the login form and the token-verification endpoint at /api/portal/auth.
fileSummaries.app/api/portal/me/route.ts: This API route handles session identity for the law firm client portal, providing GET to verify an HMAC-signed session cookie and return the authenticated firm's details (name, agent config, plan), and DELETE to sign out by clearing the cookie. It sits at the authentication layer of the portal, bridging session validation with firm-specific configuration from the Lex client registry without requiring any server-side session storage.
fileSummaries.app/api/portal/settings/route.ts: This Next.js API route handles reading and writing portal settings for authenticated law firm users, storing raw settings JSON and a compiled human-readable AI agent configuration (persona, tone, goals, language rules) in Redis keyed by user email. It sits in the client portal layer, bridging the settings UI with the Redis-backed config that the Lex AI agent consumes at runtime.
fileSummaries.lib/api-response.ts: Centralized utility module that standardizes API response formatting across all endpoints, providing typed interfaces and helper functions for creating consistent success/error JSON responses with timestamps. Sits at the infrastructure layer of the API, used by route handlers to ensure uniform response shapes and by clients to safely parse and handle API responses.
fileSummaries.lib/auth.ts: This file implements stateless session management for the Saabai Client Portal, using HMAC-SHA256 via the Web Crypto API to create and verify signed session tokens that encode a client ID and expiry timestamp. It serves as the authentication layer shared across the application, providing utilities for token creation, verification, and cookie header generation in a format compatible with Edge Runtime environments.
fileSummaries.lib/chat-prompt.ts: This file defines the complete system prompt for "Mia," Saabai's AI chat agent, establishing her persona, qualification logic, conversation strategy, and booking CTA rules to drive visitors toward scheduling an AI Automation Strategy Call. It serves as the core behavioral configuration injected into the chat API at runtime, sitting at the intersection of the AI integration layer and the website's lead generation funnel.
fileSummaries.lib/clients.ts: This file defines the client authentication configuration system for the Saabai Client Portal, loading multi-tenant client records (id, name, email, password, dashboard route) dynamically from sequentially numbered environment variables rather than hardcoded data. It serves as the central auth data layer, providing loadClients to parse all configured clients at runtime and findClientByCredentials to validate login attempts, sitting between the environment/secrets store and the authentication logic.
fileSummaries.lib/env.ts: This file validates and exports all environment variables at server startup using Zod schema parsing, ensuring required keys like ANTHROPIC_API_KEY are present and correctly formatted before the app runs. It serves as the single source of truth for environment configuration across the Next.js application, preventing runtime failures from missing or malformed config by crashing early with descriptive error messages.
fileSummaries.lib/lex-document-types.ts: This file is a registry of Australian legal document types used by the Lex drafting system, defining metadata for each document including governing legislation, required elements, jurisdiction-specific notes, drafting warnings, and search terms. It serves as the central configuration layer consumed by the `/api/lex-draft` route to guide a two-pass AI drafting process across document categories including trusts, family law, commercial, employment, and property.
fileSummaries.lib/lex-review-store.ts: This file is the server-side persistence layer for the Lex document review feature, handling all CRUD operations (save, status update, list, fetch) for legal document reviews stored in Upstash Redis using a hash-per-review plus a per-firm sorted list schema. It sits between the API routes and the Redis database, translating typed StoredReview objects into flat string maps and back, and enforcing a 200-review cap per firm.
fileSummaries.lib/lex-tools.ts: This file defines a collection of server-side legal research tool functions for the Lex AI system, enabling real-time search across Australian and international legal databases (AustLII, ATO, legislation.gov.au, ASIC, Fair Work Commission, and others) by making site-scoped queries via the Serper.dev Google Search API. It serves as the core legal data retrieval layer, providing structured results to the AI assistant for grounding responses in verified case law, legislation, and regulatory guidance rather than relying solely on training data.
fileSummaries.lib/news.ts: This file provides the data-fetching and aggregation layer for a news feed feature, pulling AI-related content from Reddit (via Atom RSS), standard RSS/Atom news sources, and X/Twitter accounts (via rotating Nitter instances), then interleaving results from multiple sources to avoid consecutive duplicate sources in the final feed.
fileSummaries.lib/pipedrive-client.ts: This file is a Pipedrive CRM integration client that handles two core business functions: looking up customer order statuses by order number (supporting multiple order prefixes like PLON-, HP-, EXP-) and creating CRM deals with associated person/organisation records and notes when Rex AI captures a lead. It sits in the service/integration layer of the system, called by API routes or server-side logic to sync chat interactions and order inquiries with the Pipedrive sales pipeline.
fileSummaries.lib/redis.ts: This file is the central Redis data layer for the application, providing lazily-initialized Upstash Redis connectivity and CRUD operations for all persistent business entities including leads, chat conversations, email nurture sequences, and LinkedIn post queues. It sits between the application's API routes and the Redis store, handling data serialization, ID generation, and list-based pagination for the sales pipeline and marketing automation features.
fileSummaries.lib/rex-stats.ts: This file implements fire-and-forget analytics tracking for Rex (an AI sales assistant) using Upstash Redis, recording lead capture events such as pricing, materials, despatch options, and email data into counters, hashes, and lists. It also provides read functions to fetch aggregated dashboard stats and manage a feedback review system, fitting into the system as the core telemetry and reporting layer between Rex's chat/lead API routes and the internal admin dashboard.
fileSummaries.lib/subscribers.ts: This file provides the data access layer for managing email newsletter subscribers, handling CRUD operations (save, fetch, delete, count) against a Redis store using hash and list data structures. It sits between the application's subscription endpoints and Redis, enforcing deduplication on signup and tracking subscriber metadata like geo-location, industry, and source.
fileSummaries.lib/url-generator.ts: This file dynamically generates product page, cart, and checkout URLs for the PlasticOnline WooCommerce store, mapping material names to URL slugs and appending UTM tracking parameters for device attribution and analytics. It serves as a utility layer in the system to avoid hardcoding URLs in the knowledge base, reducing token usage while enabling pre-filled checkout flows from the Rex quote assistant.
fileSummaries.lib/woo-client.ts: This file is a WooCommerce API client for the Plastics Online (PLON) e-commerce store, providing authenticated access to product search, variation fetching, order retrieval, and cut-to-size price calculation via both the WooCommerce REST API and a custom PLON pricing endpoint. It serves as the core data-access layer between the AI assistant system and the store's backend, encapsulating all external HTTP calls, auth, pagination, and pricing logic.
fileSummaries.app/api/auth/login/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route handler that processes client login form submissions by validating email/password credentials against a client registry, then either redirecting back to the login page with an error or issuing a signed session cookie and redirecting the authenticated user to their dashboard or a requested destination URL.
fileSummaries.app/api/auth/logout/route.ts: This Next.js API route handles user logout by clearing the session cookie and redirecting the client to the login page via a 303 redirect. It serves as the authentication teardown endpoint, sitting at the boundary between the frontend session management and the auth library's cookie utilities.
fileSummaries.app/api/chat/route.ts: This is the Next.js Edge API route that powers the Saabai AI chat widget, handling streaming conversations with an Anthropic model (via the AI SDK) and orchestrating a multi-tool agentic loop that qualifies leads, captures visitor facts, calculates ROI, and routes outcomes to Redis storage or external notifications (booking/email) based on conversation signals.
fileSummaries.app/api/cron/mia-nurture/route.ts: This is a cron job API route that implements a two-touch email nurture sequence for leads who interacted with the Mia AI chatbot, sending a follow-up email on day 2 and a final email on day 5 after lead capture, using Resend for delivery and Redis to track send state. It sits in the automated marketing layer of the system, running on a schedule to convert chatbot leads into Calendly bookings for Shane Goldberg's AI automation consultancy, Saabai.
fileSummaries.app/api/edge/chat/route.ts: This is the edge runtime API route that powers "Edge," a personalized AI performance coach for a single user (Shane), streaming responses via Anthropic's Claude model. It fetches Shane's profile and session history from Redis to dynamically inject context into the system prompt, handles both text and image/PDF attachments, and sits at the core of the chat interface as the primary LLM inference endpoint.
fileSummaries.app/api/edge/profile/route.ts: This is an Edge Runtime API route that fetches and returns a user's profile data and their 10 most recent sessions from Redis in a single parallel request, serving as a lightweight data endpoint for profile-related UI components.
fileSummaries.app/api/edge/session/[id]/route.ts: This is a Next.js Edge Runtime API route that retrieves a session transcript by ID from Redis and returns it as JSON, serving as the data-fetching endpoint for individual edge session transcripts in the application.
fileSummaries.app/api/edge/summarise/route.ts: This Edge runtime API route handles post-session summarization for a personal coaching app, using Claude Haiku to analyze conversation transcripts and generate structured session summaries and profile updates, which are then persisted to Redis (session record, updated user profile, and full transcript). It sits at the end of a coaching session lifecycle, synthesizing insights about the user (Shane) to build a persistent longitudinal profile across sessions.
fileSummaries.app/api/growth/route.ts: Edge API route that serves growth analytics data by querying Redis for leads, conversations, or aggregated stats based on a query parameter, acting as the backend data endpoint for a growth/analytics dashboard.
fileSummaries.app/api/heygen-token/route.ts: This Next.js Edge API route acts as a secure proxy that exchanges the server-side HeyGen API key for a short-lived streaming token, which the client can then use to initiate HeyGen avatar streaming sessions without exposing the API key to the browser.
fileSummaries.app/api/leads/route.ts: This is the lead capture API route for Saabai's website that handles two types of lead submissions: chat conversations from the AI assistant Mia and manual contact form submissions. It uses Claude Haiku to analyze Mia chat transcripts for qualification and intent, then sends formatted HTML notification emails to the operator via Resend and stores nurture records in Redis for follow-up sequencing.
fileSummaries.app/api/lex-chat/route.ts: This is the API route handler for "Lex," Tributum Law's AI-powered legal assistant chatbot, which streams responses using Claude Sonnet via the Vercel AI SDK with a detailed system prompt grounded in a proprietary tax law knowledge base. It handles lead capture and consultation routing as agentic tools, dynamically enhancing the system prompt with portal-specific settings fetched from Redis, and sits at the core of the firm's client acquisition and intake flow on their website.
fileSummaries.app/api/lex-draft/route.ts: This file implements a two-pass Australian legal document drafting API endpoint where Pass 1 streams an AI-generated draft grounded in real legislation via legal search tools (AustLII, ATO, etc.), and Pass 2 optionally runs a non-streaming QA verification pass that returns a structured JSON confidence report flagging citation risks and legal issues. It sits in the backend API layer of the Lex document drafting feature, orchestrating premium model calls, legal tool use, and document-type-aware system prompts to produce citeable, jurisdiction-specific Australian legal documents.
fileSummaries.app/api/lex-extract/route.ts: This Next.js API route accepts uploaded documents (PDF, DOCX, DOC, TXT) and extracts their raw text content using pdf-parse and mammoth, returning the extracted text along with word count and filename. It serves as the document ingestion layer for the lex feature, enabling downstream processing of legal or textual documents uploaded by users.
fileSummaries.app/api/lex-leads/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route that receives lead capture data from the Lex AI legal widget and sends a formatted notification email via Resend to the Tributum Law/Saabai team when a prospective client submits their contact details. It sits at the backend intake layer of the Lex AI system, bridging the conversational widget with the firm's CRM/sales pipeline by delivering lead context including name, email, matter type, jurisdiction, and conversation history.
fileSummaries.app/api/lex-research/route.ts: This is the API route handler for Lex, an AI-powered Australian legal research assistant, which streams responses from a premium LLM (Anthropic) enriched with configurable legal search tools (AustLII, ATO, legislation registries, state law, ASIC, AAT, Fair Work, etc.) scoped per law firm client via clientId. It sits at the AI orchestration layer of the system, bridging firm-specific portal configuration with real-time legal database lookups to deliver accurate, citation-grounded legal research responses.
fileSummaries.app/api/lex-review-queue/route.ts: This Next.js API route manages the Lex supervisor review queue, providing GET to list pending document reviews by firm and PATCH to allow supervisors to approve or request changes on submitted legal work products, with optional Resend email notifications sent to the originating lawyer upon status update.
fileSummaries.app/api/lex-review-submit/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route that handles submission of completed Lex document review reports, persisting the review to a data store via saveReview and sending an HTML-formatted email notification to a supervisor using Resend. It sits at the boundary between the client-facing review workflow and the firm's review queue, bridging the AI-generated ReviewReport with persistent storage and supervisor alerting.
fileSummaries.app/api/lex-review/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route that powers "Lex," an AI legal document review service, sending legal document text to Claude (claude-sonnet-4-6) via the Anthropic SDK and returning a structured ReviewReport JSON containing risk findings, missing clauses, legislation conflicts, and scores. It serves as the backend analysis engine for Australian legal document review, supporting both incoming documents (reviewed protectively for a client) and outgoing documents (reviewed for law firm quality/compliance), with direction-specific prompting logic built into the request pipeline.
fileSummaries.app/api/mission-control/health/route.ts: This is a health check endpoint for the Mission Control system that verifies the presence of required third-party API keys (ElevenLabs, HeyGen, Resend, and Claude/OpenAI) and returns their configuration status as boolean flags. It runs on the edge runtime and serves as a diagnostic tool to confirm that all external service integrations are properly configured before use.
fileSummaries.app/api/news/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route that serves aggregated news data by fetching a combined feed of Reddit and news articles (4 items each) from the shared getNewsData library function, with a 30-minute ISR cache. It acts as the backend endpoint that frontend components call to display the news feed section of the application.
fileSummaries.app/api/onboarding/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route that handles onboarding form submissions from prospective Saabai clients, collecting detailed business information (services, quoting process, tech stack, agent goals, budget) and sending it as a formatted HTML summary email to an internal address via the Resend email service. It serves as the backend intake pipeline for new AI agent client fact-finds, bridging the onboarding frontend form with internal sales/setup workflows.
fileSummaries.app/api/pete-chat/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route handler that powers "Pete," an AI sales/support chat assistant for a plastics e-commerce business (PlasticOnline), streaming responses via Anthropic with tools for product search, order lookup, pricing calculation, lead capture, and checkout creation. It sits at the core of the customer-facing chat interface, orchestrating AI responses with live business data from WooCommerce, Pipedrive, and a custom pricing engine based on client configuration.
fileSummaries.app/api/pipedrive-debug/route.ts: Temporary debug endpoint that queries the Pipedrive API with both a full order number (e.g. "HP-5089") and its numeric suffix, returning raw deal search results to help diagnose why certain orders may not be found in production deal lookups.
fileSummaries.app/api/pulse-generate/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route that powers "Pulse," an AI-driven ad campaign generator that accepts a structured campaign brief (product, audience, goal, tone, platforms, etc.) and streams back structured ad creative including headlines, copy variations, CTAs, visual concepts, targeting recommendations, and competitor positioning. It sits in the API layer of the system, using a premium AI model via the shared chat config to serve the Pulse campaign tool feature.
fileSummaries.app/api/rex-cart/route.ts: This Next.js API route handles redirects from Rex (a quoting system) to the correct WooCommerce product page on plasticonline.com.au, resolving a cut-to-size quote (material, colour, thickness, dimensions) to a specific product URL with pre-filled form parameters. It sits at the boundary between the Rex quoting flow and the WooCommerce storefront, using WooCommerce product search to match the best variation and passing dimensions as URL params that a WordPress functions.php snippet uses to auto-fill the cut-to-size form inputs.
fileSummaries.app/api/rex-checkout/route.ts: This API route handles checkout creation for Rex (an AI chat assistant), accepting one or more cut-to-size plastic sheet items, resolving them to WooCommerce products and variations, and creating a pending WooCommerce order with full calculator metadata before returning a payment URL that Rex can send to customers as a Pay button in chat.
fileSummaries.app/api/rex-dashboard-auth/route.ts: This Next.js API route handles password-based authentication for the Rex Dashboard by validating a submitted form password against an environment variable and setting a secure HTTP-only cookie valid for 7 days if correct. It serves as the login endpoint for protecting the /rex-dashboard section of the application.
fileSummaries.app/api/rex-feedback/route.ts: This is an Edge API route that handles staff feedback about Rex, an AI sales agent, by storing submissions, running an automated AI review via Claude (called "Atlas") to diagnose root causes and recommend fixes, and sending HTML notification emails via Resend. It sits between the Rex chatbot frontend and the rex-stats persistence layer, serving as the feedback intake and triage pipeline for the internal operations dashboard.
This file is a documentation-style test specification (not an executable Jest test) for the getFollowUpScheduleTime() function in the REX leads API route, defining 8 test cases that verify follow-up email scheduling logic based on Brisbane timezone rules (before/after 4pm cutoff and weekend skipping to Monday 9am). It serves as both a human-readable verification checklist and exports test case data for reference, sitting in the testing layer of the leads intake pipeline.
fileSummaries.app/api/rex-leads/route.ts: This is the main API route handler for Rex (an AI sales agent at PlasticOnline) that processes lead capture events from chat conversations, orchestrates AI-powered conversation analysis via Claude, sends transactional and nurture emails via Resend, creates deals in Pipedrive CRM, and stores lead/transcript data for analytics. It sits at the core of the lead conversion pipeline, bridging the chat interface with downstream sales tools, email sequences, and reporting infrastructure.
fileSummaries.app/api/rex-pay/route.ts: This Next.js API route handles single-item checkout redirects from the Rex chat widget, accepting product details and price as URL parameters, creating a pending WooCommerce order via the internal rex-checkout API, and immediately redirecting the customer to the WooCommerce payment page. It sits between the Rex chat frontend and the rex-checkout order creation endpoint, streamlining the purchase flow by bypassing the cart entirely.
fileSummaries.app/api/rex-price-test/route.ts: This is a diagnostic/debug API route that tests the PLON price API integration for Seaboard (HDPE) plastic sheets by fetching a product and its 10mm variation from WooCommerce, then calling the external PLON pricing endpoint to verify price calculation logic against an expected value. It exists as a temporary troubleshooting tool in the Next.js app to validate that unit price, custom multiplier, and area calculations align with what the PLON calculator displays.
fileSummaries.app/api/rex-transcript/route.ts: This is an authenticated Next.js API route that retrieves a REX conversation transcript by timestamp parameter, gating access behind session token verification and delegating data fetching to the rex-stats library. It serves as the backend endpoint for displaying historical REX chat transcripts in the UI.
fileSummaries.app/api/rex-weekly-digest/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route that runs as a weekly cron job (Mondays 9am AEST) to generate and email an HTML performance digest report for the Rex AI chatbot system, sending a detailed business intelligence email to the PLON client team and a cross-client summary to internal operators (Shane/Saabai). It pulls chat lead stats from rex-stats, order attribution data from WooCommerce, and delivers formatted emails via Resend, with authorization supporting Vercel cron, secret header, and authenticated browser sessions.
fileSummaries.app/api/subscribe/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route handler that processes newsletter/waitlist subscriptions by accepting POST requests with subscriber details, persisting them via the saveSubscriber library function, and sending a branded HTML welcome email containing an "AI Readiness Audit" checklist using the Resend email service. It serves as the backend entry point for lead capture on the Saabai.ai marketing site, connecting form submissions to both data storage and automated email onboarding.
fileSummaries.app/api/subscribers/broadcast/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route that handles sending bulk broadcast emails to verified subscribers by batching requests to the Resend email API in groups of 100, with personalized greetings substituted per recipient. It sits in the email marketing layer of the system, validating that recipients are active subscribers before dispatching, and returning send/failure counts to the caller.
fileSummaries.app/api/subscribers/route.ts: This is a Next.js API route handler that manages newsletter subscriber data, exposing a GET endpoint to retrieve up to 500 subscribers sorted by most recent along with a total count, and a DELETE endpoint to remove subscribers by email address. It sits in the API layer of the application, serving as the HTTP interface between the frontend admin dashboard and the underlying subscriber data access functions in lib/subscribers.
fileSummaries.app/api/tts/route.ts: This is an Edge Runtime API route that converts text to speech using the ElevenLabs API, serving as the TTS backend for the application. It accepts text and an optional voice ID, sanitizes the input (including phonetic corrections for brand names), then attempts streaming audio generation by cycling through ElevenLabs models in order of preference (fastest to most compatible) to handle plan-tier limitations.
